original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE head-and-neck oncology, imaging, pathology, and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults older than 18 years with a neck mass; excludes children and masses known to originate from thyroid, salivary gland, mandibular, or dental pathology.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, dental, emergency, radiology, pathology, and ENT initial assessment of an adult neck mass.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for adult neck-mass duration and suspicious-history context, targeted head-and-neck examination, risk communication, documented follow-up, and education. It does not diagnose malignancy, order imaging or FNA, or generate referral.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1–2 — suspicious history, duration, and risk stratification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statement 5 — targeted physical examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 3, 4, and 6–10 — follow-up, education, and diagnostic workup context
exact_sections.locator: official guideline key action statements 3, 4, and 6–10
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording patient education, questions, planned follow-up, clinician concern, and already completed imaging or pathology context. Antibiotics, imaging, FNA, endoscopy, and referral decisions are not mapped.

title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults
publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE head-and-neck oncology, imaging, pathology, and referral adaptation
population: Adults older than 18 years with a neck mass; excludes children and masses known to originate from thyroid, salivary gland, mandibular, or dental pathology.
intended_setting: Primary, dental, emergency, radiology, pathology, and ENT initial assessment of an adult neck mass.
